title: NOC 51114 – Translators, Terminologists, and Interpreters | GTR Immigration
description: Translators, Terminologists, and Interpreters under NOC 51114 are professional language specialists responsible for translating, interpreting, and managing multilingual communication across Canada. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation typically requires a university degree and advanced linguistic proficiency. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5111 – Writing, translating and related communications professionalsNOC Code51114

duties: Translate written materials such as reports, legal documents, and technical texts
Maintain original content, context, and style
Identify and standardize terminology in specialized fields
Provide consultative services to language professionals
Interpret oral communication simultaneously, consecutively, or in whispered format
Provide interpretation services in courts and tribunals
Interpret for individuals or small groups in various settings
Translate between sign language (ASL or LSQ) and spoken language
Provide interpretation in educational, legal, and community settings
employment_requirements: A university degree in translation, interpretation, or terminology (including at least one official language) is usually required
Alternatively, a university degree in languages or linguistics plus relevant professional experience may be accepted
Sign language interpreters require specialized college or university certification
Certification by the Canadian Translators, Terminologists, and Interpreters Council (CTTIC) may be required
Membership in provincial associations may be required
Fluency in two or more languages is essential
salary_prospects: Translators, Terminologists, and Interpreters typically earn:
$37,000 – $83,700 per year,depending on specialization, language combination, province, and experience.
job_outlook: Growth in immigration and multicultural services
Expansion of international trade and diplomacy
Increasing demand for medical and legal interpretation
Development of digital localization and AI-supported translation
Opportunities are particularly strong in Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, Alberta, Nunavut, and Yukon Territory.
NOC 51114 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and skilled work experience.
Assess your eligibility for Canada PR under skilled worker programs
Guide you through credential evaluation and certification requirements

labour_market_demand: Expansion of international business and trade
With more projected job openings than job seekers, employment prospects remain favorable.
Opportunities in public, private, and international sectors
Strong demand in legal, healthcare, and government services
Language professionals play a crucial role in supporting Canada’s multicultural and bilingual framework.
